'''Sora''' was a member of the [[Alliance Military/Legends|Alliance Military's]] [[4th Squad]], under the command of [[Corporal/Legends|Corporal]] [[Jobin]]. He was also a pilot.

==Biography==
Sora and his comrades were stationed at [[Echo Station 5-7/Legends|Echo Station 5-7]]. Shortly after combat began, the soldiers realized that the [[Ion cannon/Legends|ion cannon]] near the [[Echo Base/Legends|South Entrance]] was under attack by [[Snowtrooper/Legends|snowtroopers]] and an [[All Terrain Scout Transport/Legends|All Terrain Scout Transport]]. Jobin ordered Sora and [[Grond]] to flank the attack force while Jobin, [[Roj]], and another Rebel attacked them directly. They destroyed the AT-ST and killed the troopers. The Rebels recaptured the ion cannon at the cost of Grond and the unidentified Rebel. The Rebels were able to fire the cannon, disabling an [[Imperial-class Star Destroyer/Legends|Imperial Star Destroyer]], and allowing [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebel]] [[GR-75 medium transport/Legends|transports]] to escape.

Sora, Grond, and Jobin retreated to the hangars of Echo Base, hoping to find an evacuation shuttle. In one hangar they discovered [[Anakin Skywalker/Legends|Darth Vader]] and a stormtrooper escort. The Rebels retreated to another hangar, where they discovered a [[BTL Y-wing starfighter/Legends|Y-wing]]. While Sora prepared the ship for takeoff, Jobin was shot boarding it, and Roj went back to get him. However, Jobin told them to go and gave Roj a holorecording for his mother, [[Mon Mothma/Legends|Mon Mothma]]. He was killed by Darth Vader moments after Roj and Sora safely escaped. The message would not reach Mon Mothma until half a year later, however, during the Rebel briefing on [[Home One/Legends|''Home One'']] before the [[Battle of Endor/Legends|Battle of Endor]].
